python sdk_custom_loss.py
stdout 'SDK_CUSTOM_LOSS_OK'

-- sdk_custom_loss.py --
import tinker
import torch
from tinker import ServiceClient


client = ServiceClient()
try:
    training = client.create_lora_training_client(
        base_model="Qwen/Qwen3-8B",
        rank=8,
    )
    data = [
        tinker.Datum(
            model_input=tinker.ModelInput.from_ints([1, 1, 1, 1]),
            loss_fn_inputs={"target_tokens": [1, 1, 1, 1]},
        )
    ]

    def custom_loss(batch, logprobs):
        assert len(batch) == 1, batch
        assert len(logprobs) == 1, logprobs
        loss = -logprobs[0].mean()
        return loss, {"custom_loss:mean": float(loss.detach())}

    result = training.forward_backward_custom(data, custom_loss).result(timeout=120)
    assert "custom_loss:mean" in result.metrics, result.metrics
    assert torch.isfinite(torch.tensor(result.metrics["custom_loss:mean"])), result.metrics
    step = training.optim_step(tinker.AdamParams(learning_rate=1e-4)).result(timeout=120)
    assert step.metrics.get("optimizer_backend:mlx") == 1, step.metrics
    print("SDK_CUSTOM_LOSS_OK", result.metrics["custom_loss:mean"])
finally:
    client.holder.close()
